think.notes: 7 rules assessed for redundancy, weak evidence, and arbitrary weights. Key hypotheses: ATR/vol redundant (r=0.756), volume failed OOS, thresholds suboptimal, conviction weights arbitrary.

research.notes: Empirical tests ran: (1) Correlations: ATR/vol=0.756, gap/vol=0.792, gap/ATR=0.768. (2) Stepwise: 3 rules do most work but all 6+filter best. (3) Logistic regression: ATR=+0.289, avg_down_day=+0.273, drawdown=-0.228, vol=-0.056. (4) Threshold sensitivity: -12% drawdown > -8% but too few signals.

classify.notes: New rules survive all regimes. Bull WR 27.8% vs 18.8%. Bear 19.4% vs 16.9%. Sideways: neutral. Signal count -81% is the limiting factor.

design.notes: Designed 3-rule system with LR conviction. Specification: drawdown <= -10%, ATR% > median, price_vs_low < 1.05. + LR-based conviction weights.

validate.notes: CONTROLLED TEST RESULTS: A (baseline/all 7 cond)=CAGR 5.10%. B (remove vol)=3.63%. C (remove vol+volume)=4.70%. D (remove vol+volume+LR conv)=3.98%. CONCLUSION: Removing conditions hurts. The AND gate works synergistically. not_in_universe fix (+1.59% CAGR) is the proven improvement. No single entry condition change beats baseline.

build.notes: Reverted to validated baseline (not_in_universe fix only, 6-entry conditions unchanged). Entry condition removal empirically shown to reduce CAGR.
